Class OverlayWaitScreenProperties

Provided properties which are required to create IOverlayWaitScreen.

Namespace: PhiRobotics.UI
Assembly: PhiRobotics.UI.dll
Syntax
public class OverlayWaitScreenProperties : ICloneable<OverlayWaitScreenProperties>

Constructors

OverlayWaitScreenProperties(bool, bool)

Initializes a new instance of the OverlayWaitScreenProperties class with the specified settings.

Declaration
public OverlayWaitScreenProperties(bool useFadeIn = true, bool useFadeOut = true)
Parameters
Type Name Description
bool useFadeIn

true, to use the fade-in effect when a IOverlayWaitScreen is displayed; otherwise, false.
bool useFadeOut

true, to use the fade-out effect when a IOverlayWaitScreen is closed; otherwise, false.

Properties

Image

Gets or sets a custom rotating image.

Declaration
public Image Image { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
Image
Remarks

If the IndicatorType property is set to Image, the screen displays a rotating image as a wait indicator. The default image depends on the skin. Use the ImageSize property to specify the size of the default image. The default image size depends on the size of the overlapped control. The Image property specifies a custom image.

ImageSize

Gets or sets the size of the predefined rotating image.

Declaration
public Size ImageSize { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
Size
Remarks

If the IndicatorType property is set to Image, the screen displays a rotating image as a wait indicator. The default image depends on the skin. Use the ImageSize property to specify the size of the default image. The default image size depends on the size of the overlapped control. The Image property specifies a custom image.

IndicatorType

Gets or sets whether the wait indicator is a rotating image or dots arranged in a line.

Declaration
public OverlayWaitIndicatorType IndicatorType { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
OverlayWaitIndicatorType

Opacity

Gets or sets the screen's opacity.

Declaration
public double? Opacity { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
double?
Remarks

0 corresponds to total transparency; 1, to the normal state.

UseFadeInEffect

Gets or sets whether to use a fade-in effect when a IOverlayWaitScreen is being opened.

Declaration
public bool UseFadeInEffect { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
bool

UseFadeOutEffect

Gets or sets whether to use a fade-out effect when a IOverlayWaitScreen is being closed.

Declaration
public bool UseFadeOutEffect { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
bool
